mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market cooling that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction, which forces equity price adjustments downward within one month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with accuracy below 50% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth crossing above 2% month-over-month signals supply-side overheating that triggers short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >70% of periods following INDPRO MoM growth crossing 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate creates a negative real rate environment that erodes purchasing power and forces equity deleveraging over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods yield positive quarterly returns in >60% of cases across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll employment growth directly increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth crossing below 1.0% indicates severe economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDP growth crosses below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling employment signals automation-driven productivity gains that compress labor costs and boost equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/PAYEMS divergence fails to predict positive returns with accuracy below 52% across multiple cycles.
